status.label: Local research layer loaded
status.note: The installed find-user-research skill is available locally. Direct Confluence database import was attempted and redirected to Atlassian login, so this dashboard uses durable local research context until authenticated access is available.

studies.title: Market Research for both Yousician and GuitarTuna

marketPrinciples: Music learning is heavily song-led: users want to learn, repeat, remember, and play songs they care about.
Beginner guitar users need guidance, direction, feedback, and confidence, but many also want to feel independent.
The category has low brand awareness and no clearly dominant learning brand beyond broad platforms like YouTube.
Restarting is normal; many people pause and come back to guitar when life, time, motivation, or method changes.

simply.headline: Direct competitor lens from market research
simply.summary: Simply Guitar is called out as a growing reference competitor whose success appears tied to emotional UX, a consistent teacher character, and song-focused courses.
simply.studyTitle: Market Research for both Yousician and GuitarTuna

ultimate-guitar.headline: Song discovery and tab habit owner
ultimate-guitar.summary: Ultimate Guitar is described as a vast chords/tabs library and search leader, but users can experience it as cluttered and weak for beginner support.
ultimate-guitar.studyTitle: Market Research for both Yousician and GuitarTuna

youtube.headline: Free learning default with high research burden
youtube.summary: YouTube offers abundant free guitar learning content, but the experience is uncurated, disconnected, and asks users to build their own learning path.
youtube.studyTitle: Market Research for both Yousician and GuitarTuna

duolingo.headline: Consumer learning mechanics benchmark
duolingo.summary: Use as a mechanics benchmark only; Yousician research emphasizes guided progression, feedback, and motivation in music practice rather than language-learning transfer by default.

learning.headline: Guided learning and feedback matter
learning.summary: Yousician users value a guided, personalized path, timely feedback, progress visibility, and song-led practice moments.

practice.headline: Songs, chords, and practice flow drive habit
practice.summary: Across guitar research, the strongest jobs cluster around learning songs, chords, transitions, repetition, and playing songs from memory.
practice.studyTitle: Market Research for both Yousician and GuitarTuna

hardware.headline: Gear access shapes the beginner funnel
hardware.summary: Instrument brands and retailers matter because many beginners decide what to buy, how seriously to commit, and which learning route to try before they ever choose a learning app.
hardware.studyTitle: Market Research for both Yousician and GuitarTuna
hardware.confidence: Strategic inference from local market summary; needs page-level verification

creation.headline: Creation is a meaningful but narrower motivation
creation.summary: Some guitar users explore improvisation and songwriting, especially as they become more independent and expressive.

education.headline: Learning loops and teacher trust transfer only selectively
education.summary: General learning mechanics can inspire Yousician, and teacher/institution signals can shape credibility, but guitar research should stay anchored in practice, feedback, songs, confidence, and free-time constraints.

ai.headline: AI should reduce guidance and practice friction
ai.summary: The strongest AI relevance is where it can reduce the user's burden: personalization, feedback, song discovery, practice planning, and content adaptation.

signals.headline: Signals are inputs, not competitors
signals.summary: Investors, media, awards, and funding programmes should be treated as monitoring and opportunity inputs. They help explain timing, credibility, and strategic options rather than user behavior on their own.
signals.studyTitle: Market Research for both Yousician and GuitarTuna
signals.confidence: Strategic inference from local market summary; needs page-level verification
